message.content: Adversaries exploit Compiled HTML files (.chm) to conceal and execute malicious code, leveraging the Microsoft HTML Help system. These files, which can include HTML documents, images, and scripts (e.g., VBA, JScript, Java, ActiveX), are displayed using Internet Explorer components via the HTML Help executable. Malicious CHM files can be delivered to victims and triggered through user execution, potentially bypassing application controls on outdated or unpatched systems. Notable groups such as APT38, APT41, Astaroth, Dark Caracal, OilRig, and Silence have utilized CHM files for payload delivery and execution. Mitigation strategies include updating and patching systems to prevent exploitation, implementing robust application control policies, and educating users on recognizing and avoiding suspicious files.
